acceptance.assertion: The validator deterministically measures the freshly built bootstrap with gzip mtime zero, enforces the warning and maximum contract thresholds fail-closed, and requires committed evidence to match the fresh measurement.
Missing browser scenarios, missing blockedCatalogRequests fields, invalid booleans or negative or non-integer values, and observed nonzero requests produce distinct accurate diagnostics.
Runner and validator consume one declarative canonical smoke plan while preserving fail-closed order, required result bindings and negative tests for omitted, reordered or bypassed steps.

Bootstrap normalization tolerates whitespace, requires exactly one export assignment match and fails clearly for zero or multiple matches without changing the comparable compile-time method unnecessarily.

Repository evidence remains deterministic and sufficient for local validation; raw data is retained only where needed or preserved as digest-bound automated CI artifacts without deleting authoritative evidence.
Architecture documentation defines full-bootstrap, warning, hard-failure and measured segmentation or slim-start-model thresholds without using project count alone.
Focused unit and negative contract tests, JavaScript and Python tests, build, make validate, canonical browser smoke, catalog measurement and unchanged-surface checks pass on the exact final head.

Closeout records exact base, head, diff and merge identities, green CI including CodeQL, Pages deployment and production readback proving zero startup project JSON requests, the build-bound marker and continued public JSON availability.

approval.note: Implement in an isolated clean checkout; preserve public JSON, SEO and no-JS surfaces; bind review, browser evidence and deployment readback to exact immutable identities.

goal: Harden the build-bound Commonworld catalogue delivery contract with fresh bootstrap budget measurement, precise failure diagnostics, a canonical browser-smoke plan, robust compile measurement, bounded evidence and documented migration thresholds without changing the public product surface.

does_not_establish: permission to change the visible Commonworld product
permission to remove public project JSON files or no-JS output
permission to weaken smoke ordering or fresh-result binding
permission to reuse T019 review evidence after any diff change
metadata.source: ChatGPT operator request: Commonworld catalog delivery hardening after T019 review

title: Harden catalog delivery budget and smoke orchestration
